userStories.title: Project Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a properly configured Spring Boot Maven project so that I have a working foundation to build the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Maven pom.xml targets Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.x parent
Dependencies included: spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-validation, springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui
Application starts on port 8080 with no errors
Main application class with @SpringBootApplication present
application.properties configured with app name
mvn clean install passes on a fresh checkout
userStories.completionNotes: Fixed pom.xml: replaced spring-boot-starter-data-jpa with spring-boot-starter-web + spring-boot-starter-validation, replaced spring-boot-starter-parent 4.1.0 with 3.4.1 to match Java 21 + Spring Boot 3.x requirement, replaced spring-boot-starter-data-jpa-test with spring-boot-starter-test. mvn compile/test/verify pass.

userStories.title: Employee Model and In-Memory Repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an Employee model and in-memory repository so that employee data can be stored, retrieved, and managed across requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee class with fields: id (Long), firstName (String), lastName (String), email (String)
EmployeeRepository interface defining: save, findById, findAll, deleteById, existsByEmail methods
InMemoryEmployeeRepository implementation using ConcurrentHashMap with thread-safe auto-increment ID
Repository registered as a Spring @Repository bean
EmployeeNotFoundException (extends RuntimeException) with message including the missing ID
Unit tests for repository CRUD operations
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
userStories.completionNotes: Added Employee model (model/Employee.java), EmployeeRepository interface, InMemoryEmployeeRepository (ConcurrentHashMap + AtomicLong id), and EmployeeNotFoundException. 8 unit tests added, all passing.

userStories.title: Create Employee — POST /employees
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to create a new employee so that employee records can be added to the system.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /employees accepts JSON: { "firstName": "Jane", "lastName": "Doe", "email": "jane@example.com" }
Returns 201 Created with the created employee including generated id
Validates: firstName and lastName non-blank, email is valid format and unique
Returns 400 Bad Request with standard error response on validation failure
Returns 409 Conflict if email already exists
Unit tests cover: success case, blank field validation, invalid email, duplicate email
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
userStories.completionNotes: Added EmployeeService (create with duplicate-email check), EmployeeController (POST /employees), DuplicateEmailException, and a starter GlobalExceptionHandler (409 for duplicate email; 400 for bean validation handled by Spring default). Unit tests for service, MockMvc integration tests for controller (success, blank field, invalid email, duplicate email). mvn compile/test/verify/clean install all pass.

userStories.title: Get All Employees — GET /employees
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to retrieve all employees so that I can list all records in the system.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /employees returns 200 OK with a JSON array of all employees
Returns empty array [] (not null or 404) when no employees exist
Response is consistently ordered by id ascending
Unit tests cover: empty store, single employee, multiple employees
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
userStories.completionNotes: Added EmployeeService.getAllEmployees() (delegates to repository.findAll(), already sorted by id) and GET /employees in EmployeeController returning 200 with the list. Unit tests for service (empty/ordered list via Mockito) and a new @WebMvcTest+@MockitoBean EmployeeGetAllControllerTest covering empty/single/multiple scenarios in isolation. mvn compile/test/verify pass; manually verified GET /employees returns [] before creating an employee and the created employee after a POST.

userStories.title: Get Employee by ID — GET /employees/{id}
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to retrieve a specific employee by ID so that I can view an individual record.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /employees/{id} returns 200 OK with the employee JSON when found
Returns 404 Not Found with standard error response when ID does not exist
{id} must be a valid Long; invalid types return 400 Bad Request
Unit tests cover: found, not found, invalid ID type
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
userStories.completionNotes: Added EmployeeService.getEmployeeById(id) (delegates to repository.findById, throws EmployeeNotFoundException if absent) and GET /employees/{id} in EmployeeController. Added an EmployeeNotFoundException handler to GlobalExceptionHandler returning 404 with {status, error, message}. Invalid path variable types (e.g. /employees/abc) already return 400 via Spring's default MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException handling, no custom handler needed. Unit tests for service (found/not found) and a new @WebMvcTest EmployeeGetByIdControllerTest (found, not found, invalid id type). mvn compile/test/verify pass; manually verified all three scenarios plus Swagger/OpenAPI docs against a running instance.

userStories.title: Update Employee — PUT /employees/{id}
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to update an existing employee's details so that records can be kept current.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /employees/{id} accepts same JSON body as create
Returns 200 OK with the updated employee on success
Returns 404 Not Found when employee ID does not exist
Applies same validation as create (non-blank fields, valid email)
Email uniqueness check excludes the current employee (employee can keep same email)
Returns 400 Bad Request on validation failure, 409 Conflict on duplicate email with another employee
Unit tests cover: success, not found, blank field, duplicate email with different employee
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
userStories.completionNotes: Added EmployeeRepository.existsByEmailAndIdNot(email, id) (and InMemoryEmployeeRepository impl) so email-uniqueness checks can exclude the employee being updated. Added EmployeeService.updateEmployee(id, employee): looks up the existing employee (throws EmployeeNotFoundException if absent), checks existsByEmailAndIdNot for conflicts (throws DuplicateEmailException), then sets the path id on the body and saves. Added PUT /employees/{id} in EmployeeController with @Valid @RequestBody. Unit tests for repository (existsByEmailAndIdNot same/different employee) and service (success, not found, duplicate email with another employee, same-email-allowed), plus a new @WebMvcTest+@MockitoBean EmployeeUpdateControllerTest (200, 404, 400 blank field, 409 duplicate email). mvn compile/test/verify pass; manually verified PUT success, 404, and 409 (email held by another employee) against a running instance, plus confirmed the new endpoint appears in /v3/api-docs and Swagger UI.

userStories.title: Delete Employee — DELETE /employees/{id}
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to delete an employee by ID so that records can be removed from the system.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /employees/{id} returns 204 No Content on successful deletion
Returns 404 Not Found with standard error response when ID does not exist
After deletion, GET /employees/{id} returns 404
Unit tests cover: success, not found
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes

userStories.title: Global Exception Handling
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want consistent, predictable error responses across all endpoints so that I can handle errors reliably in client code.
acceptanceCriteria: @RestControllerAdvice global exception handler class present
Handles EmployeeNotFoundException → 404 Not Found
Handles MethodArgumentNotValidException (Bean Validation) → 400 Bad Request listing all field errors in message
Handles duplicate email conflict → 409 Conflict
Handles MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException (bad path variable type) → 400 Bad Request
All responses use standard error format: { timestamp, status, error, message, path }
Unit tests for each handled exception type using MockMvc
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes

userStories.title: OpenAPI / Swagger Documentation
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want interactive Swagger UI documentation so that I can explore and test all endpoints without additional tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: Swagger UI accessible at /swagger-ui.html
OpenAPI JSON spec available at /v3/api-docs
API info configured: title 'Employee Management API', version '1.0.0', description present
All 5 endpoints annotated with @Operation(summary = ...) and @ApiResponse annotations
Request and response body schemas visible in Swagger UI
Validation constraints visible in schema (e.g. @NotBlank, @Email)
mvn clean install passes
mvn test passes
